page.title: Channels
page.subtitle: Connect your OpenClaw agents to external messaging platforms and APIs.

feedback.deleteFailed: Unable to delete channel configuration right now.

panel.qrConnectionDescription: Use the mobile client or channel app to scan this code and start the connection flow.
panel.qrConnectionHint: Keep this window open while you complete the connection.

descriptions.media: Broadcast and public account delivery surfaces
descriptions.all: All channels available in this workspace

empty.domestic: No domestic chat channels are available in this workspace.
empty.global: No global chat channels are available in this workspace.
empty.media: No media account channels are available in this workspace.
empty.all: No chat channels are available in this workspace.

contextVisibility.label: Context Visibility
contextVisibility.helpText: Optional per-channel context visibility policy, for example quote, none, or allowlist_quote.

sdkworkchat.name: SDKWORK Official Account
sdkworkchat.description: Use the built-in SDKWORK official account delivery path so OpenClaw can hand off conversations without extra credential setup.

setupSteps.0: Open the integrated SDKWORK official account experience or install the SDKWORK client if this machine has not signed in yet.
setupSteps.1: Sign in with your SDKWORK account so the default media channel is ready immediately.
setupSteps.2: Keep this channel enabled when the current runtime should hand off into the SDKWORK official account.

wechat.name: WeChat
wechat.description: Connect WeChat conversational messaging so OpenClaw can serve China-facing direct chat surfaces.

setupSteps.0: Prepare the WeChat integration entry approved for your runtime deployment.
setupSteps.1: Keep this channel enabled when OpenClaw should expose direct WeChat conversations.
setupSteps.2: Use the WeChat Official Account channel separately for public account and broadcast workflows.

wehcat.name: WeChat Official Account
wehcat.description: Connect a WeChat official account workflow so OpenClaw can serve China-facing media channels.

setupSteps.0: Create or manage a WeChat official account in the WeChat official account platform.
setupSteps.1: Paste the App ID, App Secret, token, and optional AES key here.
setupSteps.2: Configure the callback URL on the WeChat side and enable the official account channel.

qq.name: QQ
qq.description: Connect a QQ bot so OpenClaw can route commands, alerts, and approvals into QQ groups.

setupSteps.0: Create or manage the target QQ bot in the QQ bot platform.
setupSteps.1: Paste the bot key and target group ID here.
setupSteps.2: Enable the channel after a dry-run delivery succeeds.

groupId.label: Group ID
groupId.helpText: The target QQ group that receives OpenClaw updates.

dingtalk.name: DingTalk
dingtalk.description: Connect a DingTalk custom robot so OpenClaw can broadcast updates into DingTalk workspaces.

setupSteps.0: Create a custom robot in the target DingTalk group.
setupSteps.1: Copy the access token and signing secret into this form.
setupSteps.2: Enable the channel after the first connectivity check succeeds.

wecom.name: WeCom
wecom.description: Connect a WeCom application so OpenClaw can serve enterprise WeCom conversations.

setupSteps.0: Create a WeCom application with bot or customer-contact permissions.
setupSteps.1: Paste the corp ID, agent ID, and secret here.
setupSteps.2: Save the configuration and verify that message delivery succeeds.

feishu.name: Feishu
feishu.description: Connect a Feishu bot so OpenClaw can receive and reply to team messages.

setupSteps.0: Create a Feishu app in the open platform.
setupSteps.1: Copy the App ID and App Secret into this form.
setupSteps.2: Add the event callback URL from your OpenClaw deployment if needed.

telegram.name: Telegram
telegram.description: Use a Telegram bot token to bring OpenClaw into direct messages or group chats.

setupSteps.0: Create a bot with BotFather and copy the bot token.
setupSteps.1: Optionally set a webhook URL if Telegram should push events to your host.
setupSteps.2: Enable the channel after the required credentials are filled.

errorPolicy.label: Error Policy
errorPolicy.helpText: Optional Telegram delivery error policy, for example retry or disable.

whatsapp.name: WhatsApp
whatsapp.description: Manage optional WhatsApp access rules for direct-message allowlists and group delivery behavior.

setupSteps.0: Authenticate the OpenClaw WhatsApp channel with the official CLI or runtime login flow.
setupSteps.1: Optionally restrict allowed direct-message senders or define per-group behavior here.
setupSteps.2: Keep the channel enabled so runtime login state can be reused without extra config wiring.

allowFrom.label: Allow From
allowFrom.helpText: Optional allowlist of direct-message senders. Enter one phone number per line or a JSON array.

groups.label: Groups
groups.helpText: Optional JSON object of WhatsApp group rules. Use "*" to define defaults for all groups.

discord.name: Discord
discord.description: Attach OpenClaw to a Discord bot for server and DM conversations.

setupSteps.0: Create a Discord application and bot in the developer portal.
setupSteps.1: Paste the bot token here and invite the bot to your server.
setupSteps.2: Turn the channel on once the token has been validated.

slack.name: Slack
slack.description: Configure bot and app tokens so OpenClaw can work inside Slack workspaces.

setupSteps.0: Create or open your Slack app and install it to the target workspace.
setupSteps.1: Paste the bot token and app token below.
setupSteps.2: Add a signing secret if your workspace uses slash commands or events.

googlechat.name: Google Chat
googlechat.description: Provide Google Chat service account or reference details for enterprise workspace delivery.

setupSteps.0: Create a Google Chat app and service account.
setupSteps.1: Provide either the inline service account JSON or a service account reference.
setupSteps.2: Fill audience or webhook information if your deployment requires it.
